articles.title: Gunfire erupts at Wilmington Delaware hospital; One dead, search underway for suspect
articles.description: A hospital in Delaware was locked down Tuesday after two people were shot inside, including one killed, police said. The shooting was reported about 3:30 pm at Wilmington Hospital, said David Karas, the chief administrative officer at the Wilmington Police Department.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Princess Diana: 'The greatest problem in the world today is...' Life lessons on empathy, action, vulnerability, kindness and compassion
articles.description: Quote of the Day by Princess Diana continues to inspire people through its message on empathy, compassion, kindness, action, and understanding. Princess Diana said, “The greatest problem in the world today is intolerance. Everyone is so intolerant of each other.” The quote remains relevant in modern society and encourages people to listen, respect differences, and build stronger human connections through everyday actions and compassion.

articles.title: Who is Emma Raducanu’s rumored boyfriend? Mystery man seen with tennis star after Queen’s final

Friend works in corporate public relations and is not connected to professional tennis. Neither has confirmed a relationship, but the public outing has led to widespread speculation about Raducanu’s personal life.

articles.title: The Hormuz secret Trump isn’t talking about: How long will IRGC mines keep trade hostage?
articles.description: The Strait of Hormuz may reopen soon after the expected U.S.-Iran peace agreement, but experts warn that a full return to normal shipping could take weeks. Concerns remain over the possible presence of naval mines, with specialists estimating that clearing key sea lanes and establishing safe navigation corridors may require 40 to 50 days. Nearly 20% of global energy supplies transit through Hormuz, making any disruption a major risk for oil markets and global trade. Britain, France, and Germany have already prepared warships and minesweepers for potential clearance operations, while shipping companies and insurers continue to monitor security conditions closely.

articles.title: Iran Can Now Shut Down Strait of Hormuz Anytime, US Intel Raises Alarm | Watch
articles.description: A stunning U.S. intelligence assessment has shattered long-held assumptions about American naval dominance in the Middle East. Classified findings revealed by senior national security sources conclude that Iran now possesses the definitive capability to completely shut down the Strait of Hormuz—the world's most vital energy corridor. This shifting balance of power has forced a raw strategic reality on Washington, with Pentagon planners acknowledging that attempting to forcibly secure the narrow chokepoint carries catastrophic risks. As both sides prepare for the Geneva signing ceremony, Tehran is framing this moment domestically as proof that its low-cost, asymmetric coastal defense grid has successfully forced the Trump administration to negotiate on equal terms.

articles.title: DH Toon | Too obscene for a progressive society
Government: No cough syrup sale without prescription
articles.description: Cough syrups will now require a doctor's prescription as the Centre has removed them from a decades-old exemption. This move, following concerns over contaminated syrups, brings all formulations under regular regulatory oversight. The change aims to enhance accountability and public health safety in the distribution of these commonly used medicines.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by all-time best-known American film critic Roger Ebert: 'Life always has an unhappy ending, but...'- A powerful life lesson on why happiness matters more than finding perfect meaning by the first person to receive a Pulitzer Prize for film criticism
articles.description: Roger Ebert, the renowned film critic, offered a powerful life lesson. He suggested that life's journey, filled with joy and simple pleasures, holds more value than an unending quest for profound meaning. Ebert's perspective encourages appreciating present moments. His own life, marked by challenges, exemplified finding happiness amidst struggles. This wisdom resonates for everyone living their own story.

articles.title: Chhattisgarh: Electricity Tariffs Increased Across the State
articles.description: Electricity has become more expensive in Chhattisgarh after the Chhattisgarh State Electricity Regulatory Commission (CSERC) announced revised power tariffs. Under the new rates, domestic consumers will have to pay an additional 30 to 50 paise per unit of electricity, increasing the financial burden on households. The tariff revision is expected to impact a large number of residents and add to existing inflationary pressures. With higher electricity bills set to take effect, consumers across the state are likely to feel the impact on their monthly expenses as the revised rates come into force.

Understanding these cosmic timelines provides insight into personal destiny. The system helps explain life's changes.
